message:
fix(grpc): cap streaming request/response buffers to prevent unbounded growth

Both StreamingRequest and ResponseStream used unbounded VecDeque
buffers, allowing a flooding client or a fast producer to grow the
buffer without limit, eventually exhausting memory.

Introduces MAX_STREAM_BUFFERED (1024) as a shared constant and adds
buffer-full checks in three push paths:
  - StreamingRequest::push(): returns ResourceExhausted when full
  - ResponseStream::push() in streaming.rs: same guard
  - ResponseStream::push() in client.rs: same guard (the client-side
    ResponseStream has its own state under a Mutex)

All three paths return Status::resource_exhausted with a descriptive
message, enabling callers to implement backpressure (e.g., wait for
a drain before retrying).

New tests:
  - streaming_request_push_rejects_when_buffer_full_and_recovers_after_drain
  - response_stream_push_rejects_when_buffer_full_and_recovers_after_drain
    (in both streaming.rs and client.rs)

Each test fills the buffer to capacity, verifies the next push fails
with ResourceExhausted, drains one slot, and confirms the subsequent
push succeeds.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
